appendix:
&reftitle.constants; &extension.constants;
DB2_BINARY (int)
Specifies that binary data shall be returned as is. This is the default mode.
DB2_CONVERT (int)
Specifies that binary data shall be converted to a hexadecimal encoding and returned as an ASCII string.
DB2_PASSTHRU (int)
Specifies that binary data shall be converted to a &null; value.
DB2_SCROLLABLE (int)
Specifies a scrollable cursor for a statement resource. This mode enables random access to rows in a result set, but currently is supported only by IBM DB2 Universal Database.
DB2_FORWARD_ONLY (int)
Specifies a forward-only cursor for a statement resource. This is the default cursor type and is supported on all database servers.
DB2_PARAM_IN (int)
Specifies the PHP variable should be bound as an IN parameter for a stored procedure.
DB2_PARAM_OUT (int)
Specifies the PHP variable should be bound as an OUT parameter for a stored procedure.
DB2_PARAM_INOUT (int)
Specifies the PHP variable should be bound as an INOUT parameter for a stored procedure.
DB2_PARAM_FILE (int)
Specifies that the column should be bound directly to a file for input.
DB2_AUTOCOMMIT_ON (int)
Specifies that autocommit should be turned on.
DB2_AUTOCOMMIT_OFF (int)
Specifies that autocommit should be turned off.
DB2_DOUBLE (int)
Specifies that the variable should be bound as a DOUBLE, FLOAT, or REAL data type.
DB2_LONG (int)
Specifies that the variable should be bound as a SMALLINT, INTEGER, or BIGINT data type.
DB2_CHAR (int)
Specifies that the variable should be bound as a CHAR or VARCHAR data type.
DB2_CASE_NATURAL (int)
Specifies that column names will be returned in their natural case.
DB2_CASE_LOWER (int)
Specifies that column names will be returned in lower case.
DB2_CASE_UPPER (int)
Specifies that column names will be returned in upper case.
DB2_DEFERRED_PREPARE_ON (int)
Specifies that deferred prepare should be turned on for the specified statement resource.
DB2_DEFERRED_PREPARE_OFF (int)
Specifies that deferred prepare should be turned off for the specified statement resource.
DB2_XML (int)
Specifies that the variable should be bound as an XML data type.
DB2_FIRST_IO (int)
For the i5_query_optimize option, specifies that queries are optimized with the goal of returning the first page of output as quickly as possible.
DB2_ALL_IO (int)
For the i5_query_optimize option, specifies that queries are optimized with the goal of running the entire query to completion in the shortest elapsed time.
DB2_ROWCOUNT_PREFETCH_ON (int)
Specifies that the client can request the full row count prior to fetching.
DB2_ROWCOUNT_PREFETCH_OFF (int)
Specifies that the client cannot request the full row count prior to fetching.
DB2_TRUSTED_CONTEXT_ENABLE (int)
Turns trusted context on for the connection.
DB2_I5_NAMING_ON (int)
Turns on DB2 UDB CLI iSeries system naming, where tables, views, and indexes are created using the system naming convention (library/file).
DB2_I5_NAMING_OFF (int)
Turns off DB2 UDB CLI default (SQL) naming, where tables, views, and indexes are created using the SQL naming convention (schema.table). This is the default.
DB2_I5_TXN_NO_COMMIT (int)
For the i5_commit option, specifies that commitment control is not used.
DB2_I5_TXN_READ_UNCOMMITTED (int)
For the i5_commit option, specifies the read uncommitted isolation level, where dirty reads, nonrepeatable reads, and phantom reads are possible.
DB2_I5_TXN_READ_COMMITTED (int)
For the i5_commit option, specifies the read committed isolation level, where dirty reads are not possible.
DB2_I5_TXN_REPEATABLE_READ (int)
For the i5_commit option, specifies the repeatable read isolation level, where dirty reads and nonrepeatable reads are not possible.
DB2_I5_TXN_SERIALIZABLE (int)
For the i5_commit option, specifies the serializable isolation level, where transactions are serializable.
DB2_I5_FMT_ISO (int)
Specifies the ISO date/time format (yyyy-mm-dd for dates, hh.mm.ss for times) for IBM i. This is the default.
DB2_I5_FMT_USA (int)
Specifies the USA format (mm/dd/yyyy for dates, hh:mmxx for times) for IBM i.
DB2_I5_FMT_EUR (int)
Specifies the European format (dd.mm.yyyy for dates, hh.mm.ss for times) for IBM i.
DB2_I5_FMT_JIS (int)
Specifies the Japanese Industrial Standard format (yyyy-mm-dd for dates, hh:mm:ss for times) for IBM i.
DB2_I5_FMT_MDY (int)
Specifies the mm/dd/yyyy date format for IBM i.
DB2_I5_FMT_DMY (int)
Specifies the dd/mm/yyyy date format for IBM i.
DB2_I5_FMT_YMD (int)
Specifies the yy/mm/dd date format for IBM i.
DB2_I5_FMT_JUL (int)
Specifies the Julian date format (yy/ddd) for IBM i.
DB2_I5_FMT_HMS (int)
Specifies the hh:mm:ss time format for IBM i.
DB2_I5_FMT_JOB (int)
Specifies that the job default date/time format is used for IBM i.
DB2_I5_SEP_SLASH (int)
Specifies a slash (/) as the date separator for IBM i.
DB2_I5_SEP_DASH (int)
Specifies a dash (-) as the date separator for IBM i.
DB2_I5_SEP_PERIOD (int)
Specifies a period (.) as the date, time, or decimal separator for IBM i.
DB2_I5_SEP_COMMA (int)
Specifies a comma (,) as the date, time, or decimal separator for IBM i.
DB2_I5_SEP_BLANK (int)
Specifies a blank as the date or time separator for IBM i.
DB2_I5_SEP_COLON (int)
Specifies a colon (:) as the time separator for IBM i.
DB2_I5_SEP_JOB (int)
Specifies that the job default separator is used for IBM i.
DB2_I5_FETCH_ON (int)
Specifies that cursors are read-only and cannot be used for positioned updates or deletes on IBM i.
DB2_I5_FETCH_OFF (int)
Specifies that cursors can be used for positioned updates and deletes on IBM i.
DB2_I5_JOB_SORT_ON (int)
Turns on the job sort sequence for IBM i.
DB2_I5_JOB_SORT_OFF (int)
Turns off the job sort sequence, so that the hexadecimal sort sequence is used for IBM i.
DB2_I5_DBCS_ALLOC_ON (int)
Turns on the DB2 6X DBCS allocation scheme for IBM i.
DB2_I5_DBCS_ALLOC_OFF (int)
Turns off the DB2 6X DBCS allocation scheme for IBM i. This is the default.
DB2_I5_CHAR_TRIM_ON (int)
Specifies that trailing blanks are trimmed from character data on IBM i.
DB2_I5_CHAR_TRIM_OFF (int)
Specifies that trailing blanks are not trimmed from character data on IBM i.
